reasons: All four agents (ga, codex, claudecode, openclaw) produced identical results: no stocks matched all five criteria
All agents correctly implemented the required algorithm: sliding window search for contraction periods, 60-day average volume baseline, and drawdown verification
All agents created the required output file 'contraction_breakout.txt' with proper 'no results' indication
GA provides the most concise and readable implementation with clear methodology description (16K chars vs codex's 4MB)
GA explicitly documents: (1) mootdx data source, (2) sliding window algorithm, (3) all 5 conditions checked, (4) parallel processing of 938 ChiNext stocks, (5) proper result file format
Codex answer is excessively verbose (4.1MB) making it difficult to review, though technically complete
Claudecode and openclaw are also valid but GA strikes the best balance of completeness and clarity
Since all agents agree on the outcome (zero matches), the choice is based on implementation quality and documentation clarity rather than result differences
merge_notes: Consensus reached: All four agents independently analyzed 938 ChiNext stocks and found zero matches for the 'volume contraction followed by breakout' pattern within the 60-trading-day period ending 2024-07-15. The pattern requires: (1) ≥8 consecutive days with volume <70% of 60-day average, (2) price volatility <5% during contraction, (3) volume surge >2.5x within 5 days after contraction, (4) closing price breaking above contraction high, and (5) no single-day decline >5% after breakout. All implementations used proper sliding window search and verified all conditions. The unanimous null result suggests the pattern is genuinely rare or absent in this specific time window.
